summary: Crisp soundboard recording of an electrifying summer‑84 show, highlighted by a scorching “Feel Like A Stranger” and “He’s Gone”.
review: The Miller remaster delivers exceptional clarity and separation that many reviewers say beats other sources. Musically the concert is described as a barn‑burner from start to finish with a second set that flows with passionate heat, though a few listeners note vocal oddities on “Friend of the Devil” and a hint of fatigue after the Kansas City run.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical praise (standout songs, high energy) combined with unanimous acclaim for recording clarity yields a rating above four stars despite a few vocal/recording complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear separation; reviewers say it beats the Ashley source and praise Miller’s remaster. No major drop‑outs or muddiness reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy first set leading into a second set that “flows with passionate heat”; some felt the band was a bit tired after the KC show, but overall pacing is praised.

band_member_comments.Jerry: General praise of the band’s fire; no specific guitar comments.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned positively as a “Bobby tune” by one reviewer.
